new_classes.label_de: Gin in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von mehr als 2 Litern (KN 2208 50 19)
new_classes.definition_en: Gin as defined for heading 2208 50 — an alcoholic beverage generally produced from rectified cereal spirit or ethyl alcohol by simple or successive distillation using juniper berries and other aromatic substances — presented in containers whose nominal volume exceeds 2 litres.

coverage_explanation: The key distinguishing criteria captured are: (1) the product is gin (subclass of eucn:Gin / eucn:SpiritousBeverage2208); (2) it is presented in containers with a nominal volume greater than 2 litres (someValuesFrom eucn:LargeContainer via BFO:has_part); (3) it is produced from rectified cereal spirit or ethyl alcohol by distillation (hasDistillationFeedstock RectifiedCerealSpirit, hasDistillationProcess GinDistillationProcess); (4) it is flavoured with juniper berries (isFlavouredWith JuniperBerry). A disjointness complement restriction from GinSmallContainer22085011 is added to discriminate sibling subheadings. The legal text also mentions additional aromatic substances (coriander, angelica root, anise) as optional additives, which are partially captured by the existing FlavoringAgent/FlavouringSubstance classes but no explicit restriction is added here to avoid over-committing on optional ingredients. The note text is truncated so some criteria (e.g. minimum alcoholic strength) may not be fully represented. Coverage is therefore estimated at 0.82.
